content.value: Checks every element one by one until a match is found. Works on unsorted data but is O(n) in the worst case.
Requires the array to be sorted. Repeatedly compares the key to the middle element and discards the half of the array that cannot contain it, giving O(log n) performance.
Use low + (high-low)/2 instead of (low+high)/2 in languages with fixed-width integers to avoid overflow - good habit even though it rarely matters in small C exam programs.

Repeatedly steps through the array, swapping adjacent elements if they're in the wrong order. Each full pass 'bubbles' the largest remaining unsorted element to its final position. Simple to implement but O(n^2), so it's used mainly for teaching and very small datasets.

items: Binary search: O(log n), requires sorted data
Bubble sort: O(n^2) worst/average, O(n) best case (already sorted, with an early-exit flag)

What is bubble sort's worst-case time complexity?
